theme: stripes that hold under a stretching gradient
title: Stripes That Hold Under a Stretching Gradient
innovation_hook: Correct proportioning under unknown rescaling requires reading relative not absolute concentration, i.e. building scale invariance into the network.
strategies: absolute thresholds calibrated on the nominal gradient (greedy trap): read the concentration at the 25% and 75% positions of the visible field and threshold every cell there; perfect on the nominal embryo but collapses once amplitude / offset / slope are rescaled

relative (min-max) readout: normalise the field to [0,1] and cut at 0.25/0.75; cancels amplitude and offset (affine rescaling) but a nonlinear slope/shape-exponent change still bends the stripe boundaries

rank / positional readout (the insight): label by the cell's concentration RANK fraction, which is invariant to ANY monotone rescaling (amplitude, offset, AND slope), recovering relative position directly and holding the 1:2:1 proportions

rank readout plus neighbour smoothing and cut tuning: average over the GRN neighbourhood to denoise local rank swaps and nudge the cut points against the residual low-frequency bump bias, pushing accuracy toward the (bump/noise-capped) ceiling
